description: Tempdoc 662 — the managed-connection-budget register. The browser caps HTTP/1.1 connections to ~6 per host (Chromium issue 275955, WontFix; WebView2 inherits it). The shell's 5 historically always-on SSE streams (intent, the two advisory classes, action-ledger, indexing-jobs) routinely saturated that pool, starving the cheap /api/status and /api/inference/status polls under enrichment/ingest load (tempdoc 649). Tempdoc 662 collapsed those 5 onto ONE multiplexed connection (/api/shell-events/stream, MultiplexedStream.ts + the backend MultiplexedSseWriter). This register is the budget's anti-drift teeth: every long-lived origin connection opener in modules/ui-web/src must be declared here with a class (always-on physical socket / lazy / transient / fallback / dormant), and the gate fails the build if (a) a NEW opener is undeclared, or (b) the declared always-on-class budget exceeds maxAlwaysOnPhysical. TRANSPORT-AGNOSTIC by design (tempdoc 662 Investigation §C): a long-lived streaming fetch (a chat/agent generation, a Health surface body-stream) consumes the SAME 6-slot pool as an EventSource, so the scan covers both, not just SSE.

budget.rationale: 6-per-host cap, minus 2 reserved for the status+inference polls, minus 1 headroom for transient fetches (lazy JS chunks, icons, search/file-action calls) and a concurrent AI generation stream — tempdoc 662 Investigation/Design §F. The single multiplexed connection (class always-on) is the only entry expected to consume this budget today; the headroom absorbs the rest.

classes.always-on: Holds a socket for the whole session regardless of any surface being visible. Counted against maxAlwaysOnPhysical.
classes.lazy: Opens only while a specific surface/component is mounted; closes on unmount. Not counted (bounded by concurrently-open surfaces, which the budget's headroom absorbs).
classes.transient: A short-lived or one-at-a-time stream (a chat/agent generation). Not counted directly; its concurrency is the reason the budget reserves headroom rather than spending it all on always-on channels.
classes.fallback: A direct-connection code path that exists for when the shared multiplex is unavailable (tests, or — for AdvisoryStore — an unrecognized future advisory class). Dormant on the production boot path; not counted.
classes.dormant: An opener with no production boot path reaching it inside modules/ui-web/src today (host-driven from outside this module, or otherwise unwired). Not counted; re-classify if a boot path is added.

demandClasses.breadth: Steady-state that must stay current even while its surface is hidden (a badge/count). Design §D1's preferred placement is the poll, not a socket.
demandClasses.depth: Sub-second updates that only matter while a surface is open. Design §D1's preferred placement is a lazy stream, opened on mount.
demandClasses.event: Must be live before any surface shows it (a notification, a router input). The only demand class that legitimately earns an always-on socket — Design §D1.

demandClassesNote: Annotates WHY a channel holds the mechanism `class` it does (Design §D2's literal ask: declare each channel's demand class, not just its mechanism). Optional per channel — only present where the demand reasoning isn't self-evident from the class alone. Documentation-only: the gate validates the enum if present but does not derive placement from it (that judgment call — e.g. tempdoc 662's design-alignment review §Gap-1 on indexing-jobs — stays in the tempdoc's prose, not mechanized here, per AHA scope discipline).

scan.excludeFilesNote: The 3 substrate files IMPLEMENT the openers (new EnvelopeStream / subscribePooled appear inside MultiplexedStream.ts and EnvelopeStreamPool.ts as their own transport, not as a registrable 'channel') — excluded the same way execution-surfaces.v1.json exempts the canonical record type's own defining file. A channel is declared at its CALL SITE, not inside the mechanism.
scan.openersPatternNote: Patterns match the bare call/constructor identifier, not the full call expression with its generic type argument — TypeScript generics (`subscribePooled<StrategyState<TabularData<X>>>(`) defeat naive balanced-bracket regex matching. A conservative over-detection bias (matching a bare identifier, e.g. a type-only import) is the safer failure direction for a budget gate than under-detection.
scan.openersNote: Honest limit (mirrors check-declared-surfaces.mjs / execution-surfaces.v1.json §5): import/grep-visible coverage only. A re-modeled opener (a renamed helper, a hand-rolled fetch-stream loop not matching these patterns) is import-invisible and slips past this gate — early-warning register, the accepted norm for every presentation/connection gate in this codebase. `multiplex.subscribe(...)` calls (the 4 migrated consumers' demux registrations) are deliberately NOT a scanned pattern — they register a LOGICAL stream on an ALREADY-open physical connection; they open no new socket, so they are out of scope for a connection-budget register by construction, not an oversight.

channels.note: THE shared multiplexed connection (tempdoc 662) carrying intent, the two advisory classes, action-ledger, and indexing-jobs. Constructed once at shell mount. demandClass 'event' holds for intent/advisory×2/action-ledger by Design §D1's own audit (U1); indexing-jobs is the one channel on this connection whose demand is actually BREADTH (an always-mounted tray's steady-state rows), kept here rather than poll-folded for a documented reason specific to it (design-alignment review §Gap-1, below) — not because its demand class is 'event' too.

startDirectStreamForResource — used only when no `multiplex` is configured, or for an advisory Resource endpoint advisoryEndpointToShellEventStreamId doesn't recognize (a future 3rd advisory class added before that map is updated). Dormant on the production boot path: both known advisory classes are recognized and Shell always passes `multiplex`.

openActionLedgerStream's direct-connection branch — used only when `multiplex` is omitted. Dormant on the production boot path (both AiActivityDigest and ActionLedgerView always receive/resolve a multiplex instance).
startIndexingJobsBridge's subscribePooled fallback branch — used only when `opts.multiplex` is omitted. Dormant on the production boot path (Shell always passes it).
Pre-662 finding (Investigation §B): no production boot path in modules/ui-web/src instantiates CapabilitiesHandshake — only test files construct it. Host-driven from outside this module, if at all.
Same finding as CapabilitiesHandshake — bootContractEventBridge has no production boot path inside modules/ui-web/src today.
Opens only when the Health surface mounts (startStream from connectedCallback).
Opens only when the Log surface mounts (the diagnostic-channels/head-log stream).

Generic per-Resource SSE_STREAM viewer — opens only while a Resource surface with subscriptionMode SSE_STREAM is mounted. Pooled by URL, so it reuses the indexing-jobs bridge's socket if that Resource happens to be the same URL — except post-662, the bridge itself no longer uses the pool (it's on the multiplexer), so visiting the indexing-jobs Resource view now opens its own lazy socket (a documented, minor tradeoff — see the observations inbox note from this tempdoc's build).
Two pooled subscribePooled call sites (registry-resource + health-events endpoints) — opened lazily on plugin host-data demand.

consumeShapeStream — the chat/agent/summarize generation POST response-body stream (UnifiedChatView, SummarizeView, AgentSessionController, and other shape consumers). One in flight per active generation; this is the entry the budget's headroom reserves a slot for (Investigation §C/§F).
consumeShapeStream's own implementation reads the response body via .body.getReader() — same generation-stream concern as the row above, matched by the second pattern.
Plugin-host AI stream pump — the plugin-API surface of the same generation-stream concern as consumeShapeStream.
Delegates internally to consumeShapeStream — same generation-stream concern, matched by the second pattern.
Two doFetch-based response-body readers (/api/health/events/stream, /api/condition-recovery-index/stream) — open only while the Health surface is mounted.
Debug/dev inspector pane's AI response-body reader — opens only while the inspector is mounted and in use.
One-shot conversation-title generation request — a transient streaming fetch, not a persistent connection.
Agent session resume/fork/attach via consumeShapeStream — open only while an agent run/session is active in the agent window.
Plugin-host AI capability's SSE parser — the plugin-driven generation-stream surface, same family as pumpHostAiStream.ts.
References pumpHostAiStream (plugin-driven agent runs) alongside its own consumeShapeStream usage — open only while an agent run/session is active.
Plugin-host AI stream consumer — open only while the Navigate view is mounted and a plugin-driven generation is in flight.
Plugin-host AI stream consumer — open only while the Summarize view is mounted and a plugin-driven generation is in flight.
consumeShapeStream-driven chat generation — open only while the chat view is mounted and a generation is in flight.
